vendors.notes: Products organized by SKU in subfolders. Each subfolder contains images/ and videos/ directories.
Uses public /products.json endpoint. No authentication required.
Product catalog in Sheet1. Columns: SKU | Product Name | Image URLs (comma-separated) | Video URLs (comma-separated) | Marketing Copy
Folder structure: /products/{SKU}/images/ and /products/{SKU}/videos/. Product name in folder metadata.
Requires Zyte API + Gemini AI extraction. No public API available. Product pages follow pattern: /products/{product-slug}
Shopify store with public JSON endpoints. Products paginated at /products.json?page={n}
Flat folder structure. Files named as: {SKU}_image1.jpg, {SKU}_video1.mp4, {SKU}_description.txt
Password-protected catalog. Requires Zyte + AI. Product grid layout with lazy-loaded images.
Multiple sheets: 'Products' (main data), 'Images' (SKU to image URL mapping), 'Videos' (SKU to video URL mapping)
Organized by category, then SKU. Path: /category/{category_name}/{SKU}/

data_source_types: shopify - Shopify stores with public JSON endpoints
website - Custom websites requiring web scraping

tier_classification.3: Zyte API + Gemini AI extraction - USAGE-BASED COST

template_notes.credentials_encryption: All credential fields must be encrypted before storing in n8n Data Tables. Use n8n's built-in encryption features.
usage_instructions: 1. Copy this template for each new vendor
2. Fill in all required fields (vendor_id, vendor_name, data_source_type, source_url, tier_used)
3. Add credentials if required for the data source
4. Encrypt sensitive credential data before storing
5. Import to n8n Data Tables via CSV or API
6. Test data source connectivity before enabling in workflows
